name: protein_quality_baseline
description: Protein redesign on the streptococcal protein G B1 domain (GB1, 56 aa, PDB 2QMT; Gronenborn et al. 1991, Frericks Schmidt et al. 2007 for the high-resolution coordinates). The program uses rejection-sampling to explore the local sequence neighborhood with ESM2-650M (Lin et al. 2023) at temperature 1.0 and a 15-mutation budget, retaining samples that satisfy ESMFold pLDDT and pTM thresholds while avoiding low-complexity and amino-acid-collapse artifacts. `protein-complexity` (max_low_complexity 0.30) caps the SEG-style low-complexity fraction (Wootton & Federhen 1996); `balanced-aa` (min_aa_frequency=0.01, max_underrepresented_count=8) flags severe amino-acid collapse without penalizing the natural sparseness of a 56 aa protein. The thresholds are tuned for short-protein redesign; for full-length protein redesign use the constraint defaults (min_aa_frequency=0.02, max_underrepresented_count=3). Canonical baseline for the protein-quality teaching example.
